mandatory_deductions.reason: S06 bottom bar text partially cut off at slide edge; S09 'NEXT STEP' CTA content truncated below visible area
S09 final slide CTA text ('다음 단계: 파일럿 1회 워크숍 함께 기획') not fully visible — NEXT STEP label shown but action content cut off or missing
S03: '90%→60%' stat without timeframe/methodology; S07: ₩93M annual revenue with no cost/margin breakdown; S09: ₩200K/team pricing with no cost structure visible → -0.5 × 3 slides

slides.first_impression: Bold white title on navy, workshop photo on right, 4-stat bar at bottom. Professional and grounded.
strengths: Workshop photo immediately establishes credibility — this isn't vaporware, it's been done
Bottom stat bar (4시간, 1:1, 9개, 93%) gives a 3-second executive summary
Orange accent coral line on left + bottom bar creates strong visual frame
Partner line 'Filamentree × 동아일보' positioned correctly for a proposal
issues: Title wraps to 2 lines ('Future AI Leader's / Academy') — at projection distance the line break mid-phrase reads awkwardly
Subtitle '코딩하는 아이에서...' and '부모와 함께 만드는...' are two separate gray lines that compete rather than forming a single clear tagline
The Korean subtitle '미래 AI 리더 아카데미' duplicates the English title without adding information — wasted real estate for a 동아일보 임원 who reads Korean natively
slides.suggestion: Consolidate to a single Korean subtitle: 'AI를 지휘하는 아이로 — 부모와 함께 만드는 4시간' and drop the duplicate Korean translation. This frees space for the English title to fit on one line.

slides.first_impression: Bar chart growing left to right, $43B highlighted in orange. Two stat cards on right (10×, 93%). Immediately reads: big market, growing fast.
strengths: Bar chart with year-by-year values makes the CAGR 41% tangible — 동아일보 임원 can trace the growth visually
Title '시장 기회: $43B' puts the key number in the headline — passes the 3-second test perfectly
Right-side cards with sources (교육부/GII, 한국경제/입소스) build institutional trust
Color usage: orange for 2030 target bar vs gray for historical years — draws eye to the opportunity
issues: The '$6.9B' label at the leftmost bar has 'B' on a second line — minor text wrapping artifact
'한국 코딩교육 시장 성장' card header is near the stat_label character limit — tight fit
slides.suggestion: Best slide in the deck. No major changes needed. Consider adding a thin connector from the Korea market card to the bar chart to bridge global→local narrative.

slides.first_impression: Three-column GAP cards with bold stats (90%→60%, 0%, 34시간). Clean consulting-style problem framing.
strengths: GAP 1/2/3 labels in navy badges create clear visual taxonomy — immediately scannable
Each gap has a headline stat + supporting bullets — good visual hierarchy
Quote at bottom ('코딩 문법을 외우게 할 것인가...') ties the three gaps into one compelling narrative
Source line at very bottom (Gartner, 교육부, 한국교육과정평가원) adds institutional authority
issues: GAP 1 stat '90% → 60%' lacks context: 60% of WHAT by WHEN? A 동아일보 임원 will ask 'says who?' — the Gartner source at bottom footnote is too far from the stat
GAP 2 stat '0%' for '기존 학원 부모 참여율' — is this a survey result or an assertion? No source attribution on the stat itself
GAP 3 '34시간 연간 학교 정보교육' needs comparison: 34 hours vs. what benchmark? Without 'vs 수학 200시간' it lacks urgency
slides.suggestion: Add inline micro-sources next to each stat: '90%→60% (Gartner 2028)', '0% (학원 2,000곳 조사)', '34시간 (vs 수학 200시간)'. Makes each card self-contained for the executive who won't read footnotes.

slides.first_impression: Diagram: Child (Visionary) → AI Executor ← Parent (Integrator). Three competency cards at bottom.
strengths: The visual metaphor of child and parent flanking AI is immediately intuitive — excellent concept visualization
Concrete example quotes ('하늘 나는 고양이를 만들래!', '날개는 어떤 버튼으로 펴?') make the concept tangible and relatable
Three competency cards (맥락 설계, 의사결정, 협업 리더십) with English subtitles add educational credibility
issues: TWO distinct concepts on one slide: (1) the co-founding model diagram and (2) three core competencies. Violates one-message-per-slide — a 50대 임원 must process two frameworks simultaneously
Bottom section '핵심 역량 3가지' competes with the main diagram for attention. At 3m projection distance, the bottom cards' body text is too small to read
The slide has no headline stat or key number — unlike every other content slide, there's no number to anchor the executive's memory
slides.suggestion: Split into two slides: S04a = Co-founding model diagram only (enlarge '하나의 팀 = 하나의 스타트업' callout), S04b = Three competencies as full-width cards. If deck must stay at 9 slides, drop the bottom competency section and integrate competency names as labels inside the main diagram.

slides.first_impression: Timeline bar at top with proportional widths, four columns for 4 phases. Time allocation (40/50/90/60분) prominently displayed.
strengths: Timeline bar with proportional widths makes the 90-minute core phase visually dominant — communicates where the real value is
Part labels with progressive Korean names (관점의 전환 → 협업과 조율 → 지휘와 소통 → 성취와 비전) tell a story
Bottom callout bar '10초 피드백 루프: AI 지시 → 즉시 결과 → 수정 → 반복' is the most memorable pedagogical takeaway
issues: Part 4 '핵심 포인트' shows '최고창의/협업/기술/스토리/M' with 'VP' on the next line — 'MVP' is split across lines, creating a confusing read at projection distance
Each column has 6-8 lines of text — at 3m viewing distance (deck.yaml spec), the '핵심 포인트' subsections are too small for 50대 executives to read
Part 3 (90분, the longest phase) only shows 2 activity lines vs Part 1's 3 lines — the longest phase is the least detailed, which feels under-explained to an inquisitive executive
slides.suggestion: Fix MVP text split by shortening category names. Increase Part 3 content to match its time prominence — add at least one more detail line about what happens in the core 90-minute block.

slides.first_impression: Two-panel layout: what 동아일보 provides (left) vs what they get (right). Green '추가비용 없음' badge in center. Competitor callout at top.
strengths: The '추가비용 없음' badge is perfectly positioned — the first thing an executive fears is a budget ask, and this neutralizes it immediately
Left panel uses clear category labels (브랜드, 공간, 홍보, 네트워크) that map to existing 동아일보 assets — shows you understand their operation
Top competitor callout (조선일보 에듀조선 running K-12 programs) creates urgency — brilliant framing for a media executive worried about falling behind
Right panel '동아일보 수익' items directly answer 'what's in it for us'
issues: Bottom bar text '미디어 프론티어(성인) · 동아이지에듀(초중고 구독) · DBR에듀(직장인) → 초중등+학부모 유료 AI 교육 = 없음' is partially cut off at the slide bottom edge — this is the strategic punch line showing the portfolio gap and it's INVISIBLE
No revenue share numbers or percentage split visible — a 동아일보 임원 will immediately ask '수익 배분은 어떻게?'
Right panel shows benefits but no quantification: HOW MUCH new revenue? WHEN does it start generating?
slides.suggestion: URGENT: Ensure bottom bar text is fully visible — it's the single most compelling argument on this slide (showing the portfolio gap). Add a revenue share indicator (e.g., '수익 분배: 동아 30%'). Quantify: '신규 매출 → 연 ₩28M (동아 몫 추정)'.

slides.first_impression: Three-phase roadmap (파일럿→확산→스케일업) with phase cards, KPI highlights in orange, and bottom stat bar.
strengths: Phase numbering (1, 2, 3) with period labels (1~2개월, 3~6개월, 6~12개월) is immediately scannable
KPI boxes in each card (NPS 80%+, 월 ₩12M, 연 ₩93M) give concrete targets — passes the 'how will we know it's working?' test
Bottom stat bar (15팀, 학교 3곳, 월 3회, ₩93M) provides a quick executive summary line
issues: Bottom stat bar duplicates the KPI boxes above — wastes space that could show cost assumptions or unit economics
₩93M annual revenue has NO visible calculation path: how many sessions × how many teams × price = ₩93M? A 50대 임원 will do mental math and want to verify
No GO/NO-GO gate between phases — what happens if pilot NPS is 60%? No decision criteria visible for phase transition
Color inconsistency: Phase 1 top bar is orange, Phases 2-3 are navy — the visual reason for this distinction is unclear
slides.suggestion: Replace the redundant bottom stat bar with unit economics: '₩200K/팀 × 15팀 × 31회/년 = ₩93M'. Add a GO/NO-GO gate marker after Phase 1 with clear criteria. Harmonize phase bar colors or make the gradient intentional (e.g., lighter→darker as commitment grows).

slides.first_impression: Four cards with real photos: Jay, Ryan, JY, and a '+' placeholder. Clean team page with colored role badges.
strengths: Real photos build trust — not stock images, these are actual team members a 동아일보 임원 can meet
Role badges (Product Lead, Tech Lead, Content Lead) in colored tags are immediately scannable
Credential lines (Silicon Valley, CERN, AI/ML Engineer) hit the authority markers valued in Korean executive meetings
The '+' card for future expansion signals growth ambition without overpromising
issues: Jay's card: 'Sonatus (Silicon Valley Unicorn)' wraps so 'Unicorn)' is orphaned on line 2 — looks sloppy at projection distance
No education/teaching experience listed for any team member — for an EDUCATION proposal, a 동아일보 임원 will wonder 'have these tech people ever taught children?'
The 4th card '+ 전문 강사풀' with '확장 예정' feels vague — weakens the slide's specificity relative to the other three concrete cards
slides.suggestion: Fix text wrapping: shorten to 'Sonatus (SV Unicorn)' or use two lines intentionally. Add '워크숍 9회 완료' credential to at least one member to prove teaching capability. Make the 4th card more specific: '교육학·아동심리 전공 강사 3명 확보 예정'.

slides.first_impression: Five content blocks crammed together: 실적, 비즈니스 모델, workshop photo, parent quote, NEXT STEP. Busy and unfocused.
strengths: Business model numbers (₩200K, ₩3~4M, ₩93M, 월 3회) are concrete and well-formatted
Parent testimonial ('AI가 어렵지 않다는 것을 배웠습니다') adds genuine emotional proof
Workshop photo with real students demonstrates execution capability
issues: CRITICAL: 'NEXT STEP' label at the very bottom with the actual CTA content cut off or missing — the most important element on the final slide is INVISIBLE. The executive leaves without knowing what action to take

The workshop photo appears to be a Notion page embed with visible app chrome (search icon, '...', 'Get Notion Free' button) — extremely unprofessional for a 동아일보 임원 presentation. Signals 'we didn't prepare properly'
FIVE distinct content blocks on one slide (실적, 비즈니스 모델, photo, testimonial, CTA) — the closing slide tries to do everything and accomplishes nothing decisively. Violates one-message-per-slide
No revenue calculation trail: ₩93M is stated but ₩200K × 15팀 × frequency is never shown. Executive math: ₩200K × 15 × 3회/월 × 12 = ₩108M ≠ ₩93M — the discrepancy WILL be questioned
slides.suggestion: URGENT RESTRUCTURE: (1) Replace Notion screenshot with a clean, cropped workshop photo — zero app chrome visible. (2) Make CTA the DOMINANT element: '다음 단계: 파일럿 1회 워크숍 함께 기획' in 36pt+ centered text with contact info below. (3) Move 비즈니스 모델 to S07 or create a new S09a. (4) Show the math: '₩200K × 15팀 × 월3회 × 12개월 × (1-운영비14%) = ₩93M'.

top_priorities: Priority 1 (S09 CTA): The final slide's call-to-action is cut off or missing. '다음 단계: 파일럿 1회 워크숍 함께 기획' must be the DOMINANT visual element — 36pt minimum, centered. This is the ONLY thing the 동아일보 임원 should remember when leaving the room.

Priority 2 (S09 Notion screenshot): Replace the workshop photo that shows Notion app chrome ('Get Notion Free' button visible) with a clean, cropped image. This single artifact destroys professionalism for a 50대 media executive audience.

Priority 3 (Revenue math): Add ₩93M calculation trail to S07 or S09 — show '₩200K × 15팀 × 월3회 × 12개월 × (1-운영비) = ₩93M' so the executive can verify the claim. Currently ₩93M appears as an unsupported assertion and the naive calculation yields ₩108M, not ₩93M.
design_principles_violated: One message per slide (Principle 1): S04 contains both the co-founding model AND three core competencies — two frameworks. S09 contains track record, business model, photo evidence, testimonial, AND CTA — five messages competing for attention.
Visual hierarchy (Principle 2): S09 has no dominant element — five content blocks of similar visual weight compete. The CTA should be 3× larger than everything else on the closing slide.
Data-ink ratio (Principle 4): S07 bottom stat bar duplicates KPI boxes from the phase cards above — decorative redundancy that wastes space where unit economics could be shown.
Projection test (Principle 6): S04 bottom competency card body text and S05 '핵심 포인트' subsections appear too small for 3m projection distance specified in deck.yaml for 50대 audience.
Progressive disclosure (Principle 5): S09 introduces new information (비즈니스 모델 pricing ₩200K) on the closing slide — pricing should appear in S07 roadmap so the final slide is purely about the ask.
path_to_next_score: Fix S09 Notion screenshot → clean photo: +0.5 professionalism → overall contribution +0.07
Make S09 CTA fully visible and dominant (remove cut-off): +1.0 story_flow (removes mandatory deduction) → overall +0.14
Add revenue calculation trail (₩200K × teams × frequency = ₩93M): +1.0 decision_readiness → overall +0.14
Restructure S09 into 2 slides (evidence + CTA): +0.5 info_density, +0.5 readability → overall +0.14
Fix S06 bottom bar text visibility: +0.5 readability → overall +0.07
Split S04 concept + competencies: +0.5 info_density → overall +0.07
All six fixes combined: projected score 4 → 6 (28→40 across 7 axes)
